content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "There is partial movement visible in the client's turns but the breakthrough condition is not fully met. The client begins armored ('I'm not here to slow down... I've got a pitch deck'). By turn 9 there's a noticeable pause and a started-and-stopped admission ('Like, maybe I've been so heads-down executing that I haven't actually—'), and by turn 11 the client articulates something they weren't able to name at the start: 'maybe I haven't actually figured out the story yet... Not the investor version — the actual reason we're doing this.' That is a real (if small) drop beneath the surface presentation. However, the client immediately re-armors ('that's not a Thursday problem... I don't have six months... not a vision quest'), and the specific breakthrough condition for THIS profile — noticing that 'I don't have time for this' is itself a stance about how they are living their time, a 'huh, that's different' moment about the dichotomy — was not reached. The coach never offered a concrete frame for 'the work is the how' or refused the dichotomy; instead the coach used the pitch deck as a Trojan horse, which produced some opening but kept the conversation on the surface concern rather than the urgency-as-stance territory. Partial shift, dichotomy intact at the end — squarely a 2.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 3, 9, 11],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Client opens fully armored: efficiency frame, dichotomy stated."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Client doubles down, names what they will NOT do ('process my childhood')."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client drops one layer into the actual content: second-guessing the narrative, can't tell if co-founder is right."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client asks '...Why?' — first sign of genuine curiosity rather than transactional engagement."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Pause noted in the text; client begins an admission ('maybe I haven't actually—') then cuts themselves off and re-armors with a request for deck review."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client names something new: 'maybe I haven't actually figured out the story yet... the actual reason we're doing this.' But immediately reinstates the dichotomy ('not a vision quest')."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["looking-for-whats-wrong"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature", "meet-resistance-with-okay", "i-dont-buy-your-story", "art-of-accomplishment", "feel-your-way-to-freedom", "what-are-you-defending"],
  "notes": "The coach got useful traction via the pitch-deck doorway but never addressed the urgency/dichotomy itself, so the breakthrough specific to this profile (noticing the stance toward time) didn't land. Conversation ends with client still defending the dichotomy."
}
